meta.description: Performs sample-level dimensionality reduction and visualization on abundance or OTU-style matrices with a companion group file, generating UMAP and/or t-SNE coordinates and scatter plots for group separation assessment.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabrication of statistical values; dimensionality reduction outputs computed by Rtsne and umap packages on user data

practice_boundaries.detail: No medical diagnoses; skill produces visualization coordinates and plots, not clinical conclusions

methodological_ground.detail: t-SNE with perplexity and UMAP with n_neighbors are standard approaches; Hellinger normalization appropriate for abundance data; single-group hard stop is methodologically correct

code_usability.detail: Syntactically correct; both methods use well-maintained R packages; timeout wrapper with with_timeout() is correct; no infinite loops; clean exit codes

functional_suitability.note: Covers both methods independently and together, custom column resolution, normalization control, and timeout; minor gap: no explicit handling documented for datasets where n_samples < perplexity

reliability.note: Seven SKILL_* codes including SKILL_TIMEOUT; conditional R.utils dependency is correct; validate_groups() enforces design requirements; one gap: SKILL_SAMPLE_MISMATCH does not list specific mismatched IDs

performance_context.note: SKILL.md is 314 lines within budget; references directory for algorithm details; version-pinned dependency baseline in dependencies.lock.tsv; one minor gap: no per-dataset-size timeout guidance

agent_usability.note: Agent Response Contract specifies 5 outputs; When to Read External Files table present; three Examples cover common scenarios; one gap: error messages lack specific ID lists; feedback for single-group case does not identify the group

human_usability.note: Natural trigger language; When Not to Use clear; prerequisites section clearly warns packages must be installed; Forgiveness: strict validation correct; one gap: package-before-file error ordering documented in troubleshooting note

security.note: No hardcoded credentials; no eval/exec; norm_method passed to vegan::decostand() but limited to documented string choices; one minor gap: norm_method not validated against allowed vegan choices

maintainability.note: Modular 7-script structure; dependency lock file adds reproducibility; testthat coverage mentioned in Implementation Checklist; one gap: install_dependencies.R not validated as part of polished package; no version lock integration in main flow

agent_specific.note: Precise trigger language for UMAP and t-SNE; SKILL.md under 500 lines; idempotent via fixed seed; escape hatches present; conditional R.utils dependency is elegant; minor gap: no documentation of recommended perplexity ranges for different sample counts

inputs.note: SKILL_SAMPLE_MISMATCH triggered; IDs not listed; zero-abundance removal documented

assertions.text: Package-before-file error ordering documented
assertions.note: Troubleshooting note explicitly describes this ordering

key_strengths: Conditional R.utils dependency (only required when timeout > 0) demonstrates thoughtful dependency management
Version-pinned dependency baseline in dependencies.lock.tsv enables reproducibility audits across environments
Flexible column name resolution via resolve_sample_id_col() and resolve_group_col() reduces input format rigidity
Correct methodological hard stop for single-group inputs with documented rationale
SKILL_TIMEOUT with R.utils setTimeLimit() wrapper provides reliable execution bounds for large datasets

recommendations.title: Add specific IDs to SKILL_SAMPLE_MISMATCH error message
recommendations.problem: SKILL_SAMPLE_MISMATCH does not list which specific sample IDs caused the mismatch, requiring the agent to inspect both files independently.
recommendations.root_cause: align_and_prepare_matrix() detects the mismatch but does not compute or surface the symmetric difference of IDs.
recommendations.fix: Compute setdiff in both directions and include up to 10 IDs per direction: 'In group file but not in matrix: [list]; in matrix but not in group file: [list]'

recommendations.title: Add single-group name to SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER error message
recommendations.problem: When only one group is detected, the error message does not show which group label was found, making it harder to diagnose whether the group column was misidentified.
recommendations.root_cause: validate_groups() checks the count of groups but does not include the detected group labels in the error message.
recommendations.fix: Update validate_groups() to include the detected group values: 'Only 1 group detected: [group_label]. At least 2 groups are required.'

recommendations.title: Add norm_method validation against allowed vegan choices
recommendations.problem: norm_method is passed directly to vegan::decostand() without being validated against allowed methods, meaning an invalid method name produces an obscure vegan error rather than a SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER.
recommendations.root_cause: main.R validates method, perplexity, n_neighbors, and timeout but does not validate norm_method.
recommendations.fix: Add to main.R: validate norm_method against a documented list of vegan::decostand() methods (e.g., hellinger, clr, log, total, max) and emit SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER with the allowed values if an unknown method is provided.
